text: {"claims":[{"statement":"The United States Food and Drug Administration published a document titled \"Daily Value on the Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels\" in 2024.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"United States Food and Drug Administration (2024). [\"Daily Value on the Nutrition and Supplement Facts Labels\"](https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels). FDA. [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20240327175201/https://www.fda.gov/food/nutrition-facts-label/daily-value-nutrition-and-supplement-facts-labels) from the original on 27 March 2024. Retrieved 28 March 2024.","entailment":"The citation explicitly states that the FDA released a document with that title in 2024, supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"2024","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The digestible indispensable amino acid score (DIAAS) in eggs and egg‑containing breakfast meals is greater than in toast breads or hash browns served without eggs.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The digestible indispensable amino acid score (DIAAS) in eggs and egg‑containing breakfast meals is greater than in toast breads or hash browns served without eggs","entailment":"The article’s title directly reports that DIAAS values are higher for egg‑based meals compared with those without eggs, providing the empirical comparison.","population":"eggs and egg‑containing breakfast meals; toast breads; hash browns","intervention":"consumption of eggs or egg‑containing breakfast meals","comparator":"toast breads or hash browns served without eggs","outcome":"digestible indispensable amino acid score (DIAAS)","timeframe":"2024","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Table 4‑7 compares potassium adequate intakes established in the 2019 report to those established in the 2005 DRI report.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"\"TABLE 4-7 Comparison of Potassium Adequate Intakes Established in This Report to Potassium Adequate Intakes Established in the 2005 DRI Report\".","entailment":"The table title explicitly indicates that it provides a comparison between the two sets of potassium adequate intake values.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"potassium adequate intakes","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"}]}
